reason: The update-crypto-policies command sets system-wide cryptographic policy via symlinks in /etc/crypto-policies/back-ends, which is a standard STIG hardening action. It does not modify partitioning, bootloader, or FIPS mode, and is unlikely to cause system lockout when using a supported policy.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Legacy applications that depend on disabled cipher algorithms may fail; Services using cryptographic libraries may need to be restarted to pick up new policy
